chapter_title.ar: بَابُ صَيْدِ الْحَرَمِ وَ مَا تَجِبُ فِيهِ الْكَفَّارَةُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 21 – Hunt of the Al-Harram and what expiation is Obligated with regards to it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: سكت عنه - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/94)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: سكت عنه - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/94)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) نَشْتَرِي الصُّقُورَ فَنُدْخِلُهَا الْحَرَمَ فَلَنَا ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ كُلُّ مَا أُدْخِلَ الْحَرَمَ مِنَ الطَّيْرِ مِمَّا يَصُفُّ جَنَاحَهُ فَقَدْ دَخَلَ مَأْمَنَهُ فَخَلِّ سَبِيلَهُ .

en.hubeali: One of our companions, from Abu Jareer Al Qummy who said,

‘I said to Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, ‘We bought hawks and we entered the Al-Harram. So is that for us?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Everything from the birds which enters the Al-Harram, from what arrays its wings, so it has entered into its safety, therefore, free its way’.
en.sarwar: 19. Certain persons of our people have narrated from abu Jarir al-Qummiy who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of our buying hawks and taking them to al-Haram (the sacred area), if we owe anything. He (the Imam) said, ‘If one takes any of the birds that when flying, flap their wing, he has taken them to their sanctuary, and should leave them there.’”
